name: Mobile Phase 3 — Web Push + Public Status
outcome_goal: Web push notifications fire when approval_lifecycle.open_approval_request inserts a pending row. VAPID keys are stored (public in DB, private in .env). A cheap unauthenticated GET /mobile/status endpoint returns {ok, server_ts, last_approval_age_s, mac_awake} so the phone can probe liveness without a session cookie. Notification preferences are per-device and editable in the PWA Settings page.
anti_requirements: Do NOT enable push for any enrollment without an active mobile session on the same device.
Do NOT store VAPID private keys in the database — only in .env / macOS Keychain.
Do NOT cache any API response in the service worker beyond the push handler's deep link.
Do NOT emit push notifications bypassing approval_lifecycle — the hook is inside open_approval_request only.
Do NOT widen writes outside the notifications module, one migration, one handler, and PWA Settings.
notes: Greenfield. Depends on Phase 2 approval_lifecycle. Enables Phase 5 PWA to receive live push events. Phase 4 tunnel probes /mobile/status as its health check.

jobs.label: wave0_vapid_migration
jobs.outcome_goal: Table web_push_subscriptions exists with columns (endpoint PK, p256dh, auth, enrollment_id FK, enabled, created_at, revoked_at). Registered in workflow_migration_authority.json.
jobs.prompt:
Write Code&DBs/Databases/migrations/workflow/196_web_push_subscriptions.sql.

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS web_push_subscriptions (
    endpoint      text PRIMARY KEY,
    p256dh        text NOT NULL,
    auth          text NOT NULL,
    enrollment_id uuid NOT NULL REFERENCES device_enrollments(enrollment_id) ON DELETE RESTRICT,
    enabled       boolean NOT NULL DEFAULT TRUE,
    created_at    timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    revoked_at    timestamptz NULL,
    CONSTRAINT web_push_subscriptions_endpoint_nonblank CHECK (btrim(endpoint) <> ''),
    CONSTRAINT web_push_subscriptions_p256dh_nonblank CHECK (btrim(p256dh) <> ''),
    CONSTRAINT web_push_subscriptions_auth_nonblank CHECK (btrim(auth) <> '')
);

CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_web_push_subscriptions_enrollment
    ON web_push_subscriptions (enrollment_id)
    WHERE revoked_at IS NULL AND enabled = TRUE;

COMMENT ON TABLE web_push_subscriptions IS
    'Per-device push endpoints. enrollment_id FK to device_enrollments — revoking a device revokes its push rows. VAPID public key lives in platform_config; private key in .env.';

Register the migration in Code&DBs/Workflow/system_authority/workflow_migration_authority.json and regenerate the Python authority artifact. Verify by applying against dev Postgres.

jobs.label: wave1_push_fanout_module
jobs.description: Runtime module that signs + POSTs VAPID-signed push payloads on approval events.
jobs.outcome_goal: runtime.notifications.web_push.fanout_approval_pending(conn, request_id) posts to every active web_push_subscriptions row. open_approval_request invokes the fanout. Failed endpoints auto-disable on 404/410.
jobs.prompt:
Install pywebpush (add to requirements.txt).

Files:
- Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/notifications/__init__.py
- Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/notifications/web_push.py:
    - _vapid_claims() reads VAPID_SUBJECT from os.environ (default 'mailto:admin@praxis.local').
    - _vapid_private_key() reads VAPID_PRIVATE_KEY from os.environ; raise RuntimeError on missing (fail fast at first fanout).
    - fanout(conn, payload: Mapping, *, enrollment_id_filter=None) -> int: SELECT endpoint, p256dh, auth FROM web_push_subscriptions WHERE revoked_at IS NULL AND enabled=TRUE (optionally filtered by enrollment_id); for each, call pywebpush with VAPID; on WebPushException 404/410, mark enabled=FALSE. Returns count of successful pushes.
    - fanout_approval_pending(conn, request_id) -> int: read the approval_requests row, build payload {event:'pending_opened', request_id, intent_kind, plan_envelope_summary}, call fanout. Never raises — log and swallow.

Modify Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/capability/approval_lifecycle.py: at the end of open_approval_request (after the INSERT), call web_push.fanout_approval_pending(conn, request.request_id) in a try/except that logs + swallows. Push failure must not block the approval being recorded.

Tests at Code&DBs/Workflow/tests/integration/test_push_fanout.py using rollback-isolated conn + monkeypatched pywebpush: register fixture subscription; call fanout_approval_pending; assert pywebpush called once with correct VAPID claims and payload; simulate 410 response; assert enabled=FALSE after.

jobs.label: wave1_push_endpoints
jobs.description: HTTP endpoints for PWA to subscribe/unsubscribe + service-worker push handler.
jobs.outcome_goal: POST /mobile/notifications/subscribe (body: endpoint, p256dh, auth) upserts a web_push_subscriptions row for the authed enrollment. DELETE /mobile/notifications/subscribe deactivates. Service worker's push handler deep-links to /mobile/approvals.
jobs.prompt:
Backend (Python):
- Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/api/handlers/notifications.py with aiohttp handlers:
    - POST /mobile/notifications/subscribe — requires mobile session; body {endpoint, p256dh, auth}; INSERT ON CONFLICT (endpoint) DO UPDATE SET p256dh, auth, enrollment_id, enabled=TRUE, revoked_at=NULL.
    - DELETE /mobile/notifications/subscribe — body {endpoint}; UPDATE SET enabled=FALSE, revoked_at=now() WHERE endpoint = %s AND enrollment_id = authed_enrollment.
    - GET /mobile/notifications/vapid-public-key — returns the VAPID public key (from platform_config or env) as base64url.
- Register in Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/api/rest.py under the mobile route group.

Frontend (TypeScript) — scaffold only, full wiring in Phase 5:
- Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app/src/mobile/service-worker.ts — add self.addEventListener('push', (event) => { const data = event.data.json(); self.registration.showNotification(data.title, { body: data.body, data: { url: data.url || '/mobile/approvals' }, tag: data.request_id }); }); and notificationclick handler that clients.openWindow(data.url).
- Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app/src/mobile/api/push.ts — enrollPush() fetches /mobile/notifications/vapid-public-key, calls navigator.serviceWorker.pushManager.subscribe({userVisibleOnly:true, applicationServerKey}), POSTs the result to /mobile/notifications/subscribe.

Integration tests at Code&DBs/Workflow/tests/integration/test_push_endpoints.py: subscribe + re-subscribe upserts; unsubscribe without cookie -> 401; authed unsubscribe disables.

jobs.label: wave2_public_status_endpoint
jobs.description: Cheap unauthenticated liveness probe for the phone and Phase 4 tunnel health check.
jobs.outcome_goal: GET /mobile/status returns {ok, server_ts, last_approval_age_s, mac_awake} without a session cookie. Hardened: no leaked internals beyond those four fields. Rate-limited by IP to 60 rpm.
jobs.prompt:
Add Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/api/handlers/mobile_status.py:

- GET /mobile/status (no auth) returns JSON:
    {
      'ok': True,
      'server_ts': iso8601 utc,
      'last_approval_age_s': int | null (seconds since the most-recent approval_requests.created_at; null if table empty),
      'mac_awake': bool (read from runtime.caffeinate_policy.is_held() — Phase 4 will wire this; for Phase 3 stub returns True)
    }

- Rate-limit: per-IP token bucket 60 requests/min. Exceed -> 429. Use an in-process dict keyed by request.remote.
- Explicit allowlist in the mobile session middleware: this path bypasses session checks.
- No other fields. No heartbeat data, no worker counts, no version strings.

Register in rest.py BEFORE the mobile session middleware so it remains public.

Integration tests at Code&DBs/Workflow/tests/integration/test_mobile_status.py: unauthenticated GET returns 200 with the four keys; 61st call from same IP in one minute returns 429; null last_approval_age when approvals empty.

jobs.label: wave2_notification_preferences
jobs.description: PWA Settings page toggle for per-device push enable/disable.
jobs.outcome_goal: Opening /mobile/settings shows a push toggle whose state reflects web_push_subscriptions.enabled for the current device. Toggling calls subscribe or unsubscribe; UI updates optimistically, rolls back on API error.
jobs.prompt:
Add Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app/src/mobile/pages/Settings.tsx:

- useEffect on mount: navigator.serviceWorker.getRegistration().then(r => r.pushManager.getSubscription()) — reflects browser-side state.
- Toggle component calling enrollPush() (from api/push.ts) or unsubscribePush() (DELETE /mobile/notifications/subscribe).
- Optimistic UI: flip toggle immediately; on 4xx response, revert + show inline error.
- Minimal Moon styling: hollow node, white on black, typographic label 'Push notifications'.

Add route /mobile/settings to the mobile router (or stub it if router not yet defined — Phase 5 will wire full navigation; for now render at that path if the PWA scaffold exists, else just export the component).

Tests at Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app/src/mobile/__tests__/settings.test.tsx using vitest + mocked fetch + mocked serviceWorker: toggle ON calls subscribe; toggle OFF calls unsubscribe; API error reverts optimistic state.
